macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic’s secret “Mythos” security model allegedly finds critical OpenBSD bug and remote exploit
items.summary: A widely shared discussion claims Anthropic has an internal AI security model called Mythos that discovered a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug and produced a working remote code exploit overnight. The report also says the model triggered emergency meetings among financial regulators and banks over AI-enabled attack risk, though the underlying details should be treated cautiously until independently confirmed.

items.title: New AI news roundup highlights Claude for SMBs, Claude for Legal, and Codex mobile integration
items.summary: A recent AI news roundup highlights several product updates, including Anthropic launching Claude-focused offerings for small businesses and legal workflows, plus a new "agent view" in Claude Code. It also notes OpenAI integrating Codex into the ChatGPT mobile app and other workspace-focused AI features from Google.

items.title: AI strategy talk says cheaper agent stacks with DeepSeek V4 could dramatically cut costs
items.summary: A recent analysis video argues that combining Hermes Agent with DeepSeek V4 can deliver near-frontier performance at far lower cost, potentially enabling always-on AI systems. The discussion reflects a broader industry trend toward lower-cost orchestration and model routing as companies try to reduce inference spend.

items.title: AI bubble concerns grow as leaders debate price hikes, model competition, and enterprise demand
items.summary: One current commentary piece argues the AI market could be entering a more fragile phase, citing visible price increases and intense competition between major model providers. It suggests enterprises may increasingly prioritize cost and reliability over raw capability, a trend that could reshape vendor choice and buying behavior.

items.title: Developer-focused discussion says on-device and localized AI are becoming more important
items.summary: A recent developer interview emphasizes that mobile AI and localized AI are becoming more common, with more processing shifting onto devices rather than servers. The conversation also points to faster model releases, including a mention of Gemini 4, reflecting the rapid pace of competitive product updates in the AI sector.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung rolls out One UI 8.5 stable update to Galaxy S24 series, Fold6, and Flip6 in the US
items.summary: Samsung has started pushing the stable One UI 8.5 update to several of its recent flagship devices in the United States, including the Galaxy S24 lineup, Galaxy Z Fold6, and Galaxy Z Flip6. This is one of the biggest consumer-device software updates in the last day.

items.title: Windows updates will soon be able to automatically fix failed driver updates
items.summary: Microsoft is working on a Windows update feature that can automatically recover from botched driver updates, which could reduce troubleshooting for users and IT teams. The change points to a broader push to make Windows maintenance more resilient.

items.title: Google limits new Gemini Intelligence rollout to select high-end Android phones
items.summary: Google’s new Gemini Intelligence feature is reportedly being restricted to a small number of premium Android flagships at launch. The limited availability underscores how Google is staging access to its latest platform capabilities.

items.title: Google changes Gmail storage policy for some new accounts
items.summary: Google is asking some users to provide phone numbers to unlock the full 15GB of Gmail storage, while others may only receive 5GB free by default. The shift has drawn attention as a notable change to a core consumer service.

items.title: NGINX zero-day CVE-2026-42945 reportedly exploited in the wild
items.summary: Security researchers say a new NGINX vulnerability is being actively exploited and can trigger worker crashes and possibly remote code execution. This is a significant infrastructure-security development with potential impact across web servers.

items.title: Grafana GitHub token breach led to code download and extortion attempt
items.summary: Grafana disclosed that a GitHub token compromise allowed attackers to download parts of its codebase and attempt extortion. The incident adds to a growing list of recent software supply-chain and developer-platform security events.

items.title: Meta launches Incognito Chat for private conversations in Meta AI and WhatsApp
items.summary: Meta has introduced an Incognito Chat mode aimed at making conversations with Meta AI and in WhatsApp feel more private. The feature rollout is part of Meta’s ongoing product updates across its messaging and AI ecosystem.

items.title: Sony Xperia 1 VIII launches amid new flagship phone comparisons
items.summary: Sony’s latest Xperia 1 VIII is featured in recent coverage alongside direct comparisons with competing ultra-premium camera phones. The launch adds another high-end device to an already crowded smartphone market.
